articles.title: The Mercedes EQS returns with massive range and charging gains
articles.description: A year ago, Mercedes-Benz did the prudent thing and paused its EQ lineup of electric vehicles in the US. With customer demand drying up for luxury EVs, and federal incentives getting axed by vengeful Republicans, Mercedes put its first-generation EVs on ice. But then, in January, Mercedes quietly reintroduced the EQS brand in the US, […]
articles.author: Andrew J. Hawkins

articles.title: Justin Bieber's YouTube Coachella set had nothing to do with who owns his music
articles.description: This past Saturday at the Coachella music festival, Justin Bieber played the first of two headlining sets in a deal reportedly worth $10 million. It was his most significant solo performance in years. But Bieber spent some of his time on stage the way many of us do on Saturday nights: on YouTube. For some […]
articles.author: Jay Peters

articles.title: RAMageddon has come for Microsoft's Surface Pro and Surface Laptop
articles.description: Microsoft just raised the prices across its line of Surface devices amid the global RAM shortage. Now, the 13-inch Surface Pro 11 and the 13.8-inch Surface Laptop 7 cost $500 more than their original starting price, going from $999 to $1,499, as reported earlier by Windows Central. Last year, Microsoft stopped selling the $999 versions […]
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.title: NZXT to pay $3.45 million settlement over Flex PC rentals
articles.description: NZXT and its business partner Fragile have agreed to pay $3.45 million to settle a class-action lawsuit that accused the companies of attempting to "scam" consumers through their Flex PC rental service. The preliminary settlement was filed in a California District Court on April 7th to close a civil RICO case against the Flex program […]
articles.author: Jess Weatherbed

articles.title: AI influencers are ‘everywhere’ at Coachella
articles.description: Coachella kicked off on Friday, and as usual, it's the place to be for online influencers looking to show off their memorable experiences at the festival. A quick scroll through my social media feeds has already shown me many uncannily attractive figures in glitzy outfits, posing for perfectly staged photographs with celebrities. Only some of […]
articles.author: Jess Weatherbed

articles.title: The Apple Watch Series 11 has returned to its best-ever price
articles.description: If you’re still holding onto an older Apple Watch, now might be a good time to upgrade. Right now, the 42mm Apple Watch Series 11 with GPS is on sale for around $299 ($100 off) at Amazon, Best Buy, and Target, which is its best price to date. If you prefer a larger size, the […]
articles.author: Sheena Vasani

articles.title: Xbox Game Pass 'has become too expensive,’ says Microsoft’s new gaming chief in leaked memo
articles.description: Microsoft's new Xbox chief, Asha Sharma, has signaled that Xbox Game Pass pricing is about to change. In an internal memo to Xbox employees, obtained by The Verge, Sharma admits that "Game Pass has become too expensive for players" and that Microsoft needs "a better value equation." "Game Pass is central to gaming value on […]
articles.author: Tom Warren

articles.title: You can make a multicolor MacBook Neo out of Apple's spare parts
articles.description: If you're having trouble deciding on a color for a new MacBook Neo, you might not have to choose just one. The folks over at MacWorld found that Apple doesn't appear to limit the colors of spare parts you can buy for your Neo, potentially allowing you to deck out your indigo laptop with pink-tinted […]
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.title: Read OpenAI's latest internal memo about beating the competition — including Anthropic
articles.description: OpenAI's chief revenue officer, Denise Dresser, sent a four-page memo to employees on Sunday about the company's strategic direction, emphasizing the need to lock in users and grow its enterprise business. The memo, which was viewed by The Verge, repeatedly underlines the importance of building a moat around its AI products, to combat how easy […]
articles.author: Hayden Field
